message:
fix(viewer): prevent global click handler from duplicating graph detail surface in graph view

- Add early return in the global bead-click handler when current view is
  'graph', so node clicks are routed exclusively through the dedicated
  graph detail pane rather than triggering both the global modal and the
  inline graph panel simultaneously
- Add viewer_runtime_avoids_duplicate_graph_detail_surfaces test asserting
  the guard clause and graphDetailNode routing exist in viewer.js
- Sync beads issue tracker and history snapshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -209,6 +209,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-54fy","title":"Support multi-repo workspace baselines","description":"Currently, --save-baseline and --robot-drift only work for single-repo workspaces. Multi-repo workspaces silently skip baseline operations (src/main.rs:2827-2828).

CURRENT BEHAVIOR:
When a .bv/workspace.yaml has multiple enabled repos, the baseline code skips namespacing and saves/loads a flat baseline. This means:
- Baselines saved from multi-repo workspaces may have ID collisions
- Drift detection against such baselines could produce incorrect results
- No error or warning is emitted — it silently operates on un-namespaced data

WHY THIS IS HARD:
Per-repo baselines require:
1. Splitting the baseline file into per-repo sections (or using a directory of baseline files)
2. Namespacing issue IDs consistently between save and load
3. Handling the case where repos are added/removed between baseline snapshots
4. Deciding whether cross-repo drift (e.g., new dependency edge between repos) should be tracked

APPROACH:
1. Save baselines as a JSON object keyed by repo prefix
2. On load, match each section to the current workspace repos
3. Report removed/added repos as drift events
4. Namespace IDs using the same logic as loader::namespace_workspace_issues()

WORKAROUND: Users with multi-repo workspaces can save baselines per-repo by running bvr with --beads-file pointing to each repo individually.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:00.870871066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:36.565920481Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:36.565121849Z","close_reason":"Added multi-repo workspace warnings for --save-baseline, --check-drift, --robot-drift. Full per-repo baseline support deferred as future feature (commit 8f2856f).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","drift","workspace"]}

Add comprehensive unit tests covering edge cases, error handling, and key export paths.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:59:58.187325218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:04:04.874663821Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T03:04:04.872987573Z","close_reason":"Added 18 new unit tests to export_sqlite.rs covering: collect_dependency_rows (filtering, dedup, sorting, empty targets), collect_comment_rows (composite IDs, cross-issue sorting), normalized_source_repo (empty/whitespace/normal), emit_bootstrap_config error path, empty issue population, idempotent population, title handling (none/blank), hash_file consistency, path helpers, and chunk reassembly. Total tests: 8→26. All 1,335 tests pass with clean clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6gv","title":"Config system: per-metric toggles, timeouts, size-based thresholds","description":"WHAT: Implement AnalysisConfig struct with per-metric enable/disable toggles (e.g. enable_pagerank, enable_betweenness, enable_hits, enable_eigenvector, enable_kcore), computation timeouts per metric, and size-based thresholds (e.g. skip betweenness for graphs >10k nodes). WHY: Go's analysis engine has configurable analysis via AnalysisOptions struct (pkg/analysis/analysis.go) that lets users tune which metrics run and their resource bounds. Our Rust engine currently hardcodes all metrics as always-on with no timeouts. Without this, large graphs can hang or OOM. IMPLEMENTATION: Add AnalysisConfig to src/analysis/graph.rs, wire it through GraphAnalyzer::new(), expose via --analysis-config CLI flag (YAML/JSON file) and BV_ANALYSIS_CONFIG env var. Default config should match current behavior (all enabled, generous timeouts). ACCEPTANCE: Config file loads, metrics can be individually disabled, timeouts abort long-running computations gracefully, robot output includes which metrics were skipped and why. PARENT: bd-3in","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:02:38.038722666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T23:11:17.452521398Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T23:11:17.452107073Z","close_reason":"Implemented AnalysisConfig with per-metric enable/disable toggles and size-based thresholds. Added SkippedMetric tracking, compute_metrics_with_config(), factory methods (full/for_size/triage_only), serde support, and Analyzer::new_with_config(). 9 new unit tests. All 669 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-6gv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN: AnalysisConfig should be a simple YAML/JSON file: {metrics: {pagerank: {enabled: true, max_iterations: 100}, betweenness: {enabled: true, timeout_ms: 5000, max_nodes: 10000}, ...}, cache: {ttl_seconds: 300}, async: {background_threshold: 1000}}. Load from file path, env var, or CLI flag. Merge with defaults (all enabled, generous limits). Serialize back to JSON for robot output echo.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:09:13Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-6gv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ADDITIONAL DETAIL from Go config.go: The AnalysisConfig has per-metric fields: ComputeBetweenness (with BetweennessMode: exact/approximate/skip), ComputePageRank, ComputeHITS, ComputeCycles, ComputeEigenvector, ComputeCriticalPath, ComputeKCore, ComputeArticulation, ComputeSlack. Each has associated timeout fields and skip reasons. Go provides factory functions: DefaultConfig() (all enabled, exact betweenness), ConfigForSize(nodeCount, edgeCount int) (adaptive), FullAnalysisConfig(), TriageConfig() (PageRank+Betweenness only), NoPhase2Config(). Env overrides: BV_SKIP_PHASE2, BV_PHASE2_TIMEOUT_S via ApplyEnvOverrides(). Rust should implement all factory functions.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:33:42Z"},{"id":95,"issue_id":"bd-6gv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes inline unit tests. Config: test load/merge/default. Cache: test hit/miss/TTL/invalidation. Feedback: test round-trip/apply/corrupt-file. Async: test fast-return/eventual-complete.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:47Z"}]}
